visual_description: A group of men in light-colored shirts and ties are seated around a dark conference table in a modern office space, with one man standing at the head of the table facing them. The standing man gestures slightly with his hands as he speaks, while the seated men maintain attentive postures, some looking toward him, others at documents or screens. The room has glass partitions, fluorescent lighting, and visible office equipment, with one screen displaying a video feed in the background.
audio_description: The audio clip opens in a quiet, acoustically controlled indoor environment, likely a small office or private room. A single male voice, speaking in standard Mandarin Chinese, is heard very close to the microphone. The speaker’s tone is subdued, measured, and marked by a sense of resignation and regret. He begins with a soft, breathy sigh, immediately followed by the phrase “对不起” (“I’m sorry”), delivered in a low, slightly breathy register. This is followed by a brief pause, during which a faint rustling sound—possibly from clothing or a hand moving—can be heard, suggesting a physical gesture of discomfort or self-reproach. After a longer pause, the speaker continues with “是我的疏忽” (“It was my negligence”), maintaining a slow, deliberate pace and a tone of self-blame. The delivery is calm and controlled, with no hint of defensiveness or, and the voice remains close and intimate throughout. The room’s acoustics are dry and neutral, with no reverberation or background noise, indicating a small, well-furnished space. The recording is of high fidelity, capturing subtle vocal nuances and environmental details without distortion or artifacts. At the conclusion of the speech, a sharp, mechanical click is heard
